title: Identifying First Person vs. Third Person

steps.instruction: Passage A: 'I walked into the classroom and sat at my desk.' Passage B: 'Sam walked into the classroom and sat at his desk.'
steps.work: A: 'I walked... my desk.'  B: 'Sam walked... his desk.'
steps.explanation: These tell the same event but from different points of view.

steps.instruction: First person uses 'I,' 'me,' 'my.' Third person uses 'he,' 'she,' 'they,' or character names.
steps.work: Passage A uses 'I' and 'my' → first person. Passage B uses 'Sam' and 'his' → third person.
steps.explanation: The pronouns tell us who is narrating.

steps.instruction: How does the point of view change what we know?
steps.work: First person: We know the narrator's thoughts and feelings directly. Third person: An outside narrator describes what Sam does.
steps.explanation: In first person, the character tells their own story. In third person, someone outside the story tells it. Checking the pronouns is the fastest way to identify point of view.

title: Identifying Different Characters' Points of View

steps.instruction: Read: 'Mom said the roller coaster would be fun. Jake thought it looked too scary. His little sister Ella could not wait to ride it.'
steps.work: Mom: it would be fun. Jake: too scary. Ella: can't wait to ride it.
steps.explanation: Each character sees the same thing differently. Let's compare their viewpoints.

steps.subgoalLabel: List each character's point of view
steps.instruction: How does each character feel about the roller coaster?
steps.work: Mom: positive (fun). Jake: negative (scary). Ella: excited (can't wait).
steps.explanation: All three characters look at the same roller coaster but feel differently about it.

steps.instruction: Why might these characters see the same thing differently?
steps.work: Mom is an adult and has ridden coasters before. Jake might be afraid of heights. Ella might love thrills.
steps.explanation: Characters have different points of view based on their experiences, age, and personality. Understanding different viewpoints helps us understand characters better.
